project.name: TUGAS-AKHIR-VAR-TATA-SURYA
project.currentFocus: Stabilize mobile AR solar-system marker layout, planet detail scale, and close/reopen lifecycle for Android, iOS, and tablet browsers.

lastAchievements: Changed the main marker view to a controlled mini solar row instead of showing the unstable imported solar_system.glb layout directly.
Increased the controlled mini solar row scale from 0.18 to 0.28 because the mobile marker view was too small on real-device testing.
Disabled visible debug hit-zones so blue calibration spheres no longer appear over the Hiro marker in production.
Aligned invisible planet hit-zones with the controlled mini solar row positions in src/data/planets.ts.
Added runtime bounding-box fitting for individual detail planet GLBs so opened planets target a small marker-space size instead of covering phone screens.
Strengthened scanner close cleanup by forcing landing shell layout restoration and adding longer repeated post-close AR artifact cleanup passes.
Added explicit landing/scanner pointer-event restoration so the landing buttons can receive taps after closing AR.
Kept visualViewport-driven app-height and added a touch viewport aspect clamp to reduce Android Chrome huge-height regressions.
pendingIssues: Real-device verification is still required on Infinix Note 40, Android Chrome, iOS Safari, and tablets after Vercel deploy.
If the controlled mini solar row is still too small or too large on the physical marker, tune SOLAR_FALLBACK_SCALE in src/ar/scene.ts by small increments from the current 0.28 baseline.
If planet taps feel offset after real-device testing, tune hitZonePosition and hitZoneRadius in src/data/planets.ts.
If close still fails on a specific browser, capture DevTools source dimensions and class list after close to decide whether a full AR remount/reload fallback is needed.
nextBestActions: Deploy to Vercel and test open AR -> scan Hiro -> tap planet -> close detail -> close scanner -> reopen AR.
Confirm the blue hit-zone spheres are no longer visible over the marker.
Confirm the main solar row appears small and ordered, and planet detail models do not block the camera.
Confirm closing scanner repeatedly returns to the full-size landing page and both landing buttons are tappable.
validationState: npm run build after scale and close tap-guard patch: PASS
